page: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the server includes 7600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6id.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem delivers top-tier performance for large-block read-only bandwidth at 339.2 GB/sec. Single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, falls into the average category. The server excels in specialized workloads, showing top-tier capabilities in ray tracing, image processing, and Passmark CPU encryption tests, alongside strong performance in software compilation and static web serving.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive parallel processing combined with high-capacity local NVMe storage. However, the single-core performance is moderate, representing a tradeoff for applications that cannot leverage its 128 vCPUs. The server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and large-scale compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores, and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It features 7600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD local instance storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth, though its single-core performance remains average. It is highly suited for database operations, multi-threaded processing, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for demanding database workloads.
